stories.title: Prada to Open Upcoming Milan Women’s Fashion Week
stories.summary: Prada will open Milan Women's Fashion Week on September 22, staging its runway show at 2 p.m. CET, marking a change from its traditional Thursday slot. The event, running from September 22 to 28, features 54 physical and six digital shows, with notable sophomore presentations from Demna for Gucci, Maria Grazia Chiuri for Fendi, and Meryll Rogge for Marni. The week will conclude with shows from heavy-hitters Dolce & Gabbana, Bottega Veneta, and Ferragamo on September 25.
sources: FashionNetwork - The World's Fashion Business News

stories.title: ‘Up in smoke’: Attacks on ‘Russia’s Amazon’ bring Putin’s war closer to home
stories.summary: Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian warehouses have sent hundreds of millions of dollars in inventory up in flames, killing at least eight people and disrupting the lives of ordinary Russians. The attacks, targeting Russia's largest online retailer Wildberries, are part of a Ukrainian campaign of economic warfare aimed at shattering the "illusion of comfortable peacetime life" enjoyed by Russian citizens. The strikes have brought Moscow's war on Ukraine closer to home, with several regions affected, including the Moscow region, Tambov region, Krasnodar region, and Voronezh region.

stories.title: Opinion | Levy: Local opposition to Flock cameras is strong
stories.summary: Avon residents have expressed strong opposition to Flock cameras, with hundreds of comments on local Facebook groups criticizing the devices and their concerns about data collection. The Avon Police Department's Facebook post about the destruction of two Flock cameras and a speed camera received over 17,000 comments, with many residents voicing their disapproval. The town is now investigating the destruction of the cameras, which is being treated as a felony, with estimated costs of over $15,000 for replacement and repairs.

stories.title: Samaria: The man who’s seen every side of the game - The Namibian -
stories.summary: Namibian football legend Frans ‘Killa’ Samaria has died at 72. A former player, referee, instructor, and match commissioner, he served the Namibia Football Association and CAF for decades. Samaria leaves behind a legacy spanning every corner of the game.

stories.title: MLB Gameday: Tigers 5, Cubs 1 Final Score (07/22/2026)
stories.summary: The Detroit Tigers secured a 5-1 victory over the Chicago Cubs at Wrigley Field on July 22, 2026, with a strong performance from their starting pitcher, who allowed just one run in six innings. The Tigers' offense was led by two home runs from their star player, who drove in three runs. The win marks the Tigers' fifth consecutive victory.

stories.title: Tyla – A*POP
stories.summary: Tyla releases her new album APOP, marking another bold step in her global pop career. The Grammy-winning South African artist blends confidence and vulnerability across 11 tracks, opening with Is It Love. The record reflects her growth while staying true to her signature intimate yet understated sound.

stories.title: Charli xcx – Music, Fashion, Film
stories.summary: Charli xcx releases her new album Music, Fashion, Film, a shorter follow-up to last year’s breakout Brat. The 12-track collection leans into tense, unsettling energy with most songs under three minutes. The lead single Rock Music drops today.

stories.title: 2026 WNBA 3-Point Contest Odds, Prediction, Participants, Past Winners (Bet on Rhyne Howard, Fade Azzi Fudd)
stories.summary: The 2026 WNBA All-Star festivities kick off Friday night at Wintrust Arena with the 3-Point Contest, featuring six sharpshooters including Rhyne Howard and Azzi Fudd. Howard, a 2022 finalist, and Mabrey, who scored just 19 points in 2024, headline the fresh field. The contest starts at 8 p.m. local time.

stories.title: US hits dozens of trading partners with new wave of tariffs
stories.summary: The US has imposed new tariffs of 10% to 12.5% on goods from 60 trading partners, including the UK, China and the EU, covering 99.4% of American imports. The move replaces an expiring 10% levy and targets nations accused of failing to address forced labour. The White House claims the duties will address human rights abuses and trade distortions.

stories.title: Angela Rayner rules out rent controls in England
stories.summary: Housing Secretary Angela Rayner has ruled out introducing rent controls in England, despite calls from some mayors. She cited the Renters' Rights Act, which took effect in May, as the government’s preferred solution to rising rental costs. The policy shift follows temporary rent controls in Scotland, which expired last year without lasting impact.

stories.title: Tech titan ordered to pay ex-wife $644m in divorce settlement
stories.summary: A South Korean court ordered SK Group chairman Chey Tae-won to pay his ex-wife Roh Soh-yeong $644 million in their record-breaking divorce settlement. The amount is lower than the initial $870 million ruling in 2024 after a decade-long dispute over asset division. The finalized payment follows deliberations on dividing assets from their 35-year marriage.

stories.title: Regional mayors to take key roles in UK policy decisions as part of No 10 North plans
stories.summary: Regional mayors will take key roles in UK economic policy as part of the new No 10 North initiative, Prime Minister Andy Burnham announced on Friday. The revived National Economic Council will meet for the first time, aiming to decentralise decision-making from London and boost growth across the country. The move marks a major shift in government power-sharing, Burnham said.

stories.title: France orders evacuation of tourist spot as hundreds flee wildfires by boat
stories.summary: French authorities have ordered the full evacuation of the Cap Ferret peninsula in south-west France as wildfires rage in the Gironde region, scorching 8,700 hectares since Tuesday. Hundreds fled by boat while another major fire near Biscarrosse has forced over 23,000 people from their homes, including a nursing home and summer camp. No new casualties have been reported overnight, though two firefighters died battling blazes near Bordeaux airport earlier this week.

stories.title: Why have thousands taken to the streets? India's 'cockroach' protest explained
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of young protesters marched across Delhi and other Indian cities on Thursday, demanding the resignation of the education minister. The movement, dubbed the Cockroach Janta Party, began as an online satire just months ago and has since grown into a major youth-led campaign.

stories.title: Man Utd bring in Leicester goalkeeper Leitzig
stories.summary: Manchester United have signed goalkeeper Janina Leitzig from Leicester City for an undisclosed fee. The 27-year-old German international joins on a three-year deal after United triggered her release clause. Leitzig, who made nearly 70 appearances for Leicester, aims to help United avoid relegation in the Women's Super League.

stories.title: ‘It was the moment I lost innocence’: how a childhood stop-and-search led to the British debut movie of the year
stories.summary: A childhood stop-and-search inspired filmmaker Mran Perretta’s debut feature Ish, which follows 12-year-old Ishmail in Luton. The poetic coming-of-age drama has drawn comparisons to classics like Kes and Apu, with critics praising its heartfelt portrayal. Perretta’s film opens in UK cinemas this week after strong early reviews.

stories.title: Scotland's Gunn concedes four in San Jose debut defeat
stories.summary: Scotland’s Gunn conceded four in his San Jose debut defeat on Sunday. Brekalo, Angulo, Griezmann and Ojeda scored as San Jose lost 4-0 to LAFC. Gunn’s side remain tied on points at the top of the Western Conference but trail Vancouver on goal difference.

stories.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
stories.summary: Coastal towns in England and Wales face a youth crisis, with nearly one million 16- to 24-year-olds not in work, education or training. Funding for youth services has plummeted by 73% over 12 years, leaving young people isolated and struggling. The Guardian’s year-long project highlights their struggles amid decades of deprivation and negative stereotypes.

stories.title: Who am I? Guess The Hundred cricket star No 4
stories.summary: A new cricket quiz challenges fans to guess the identity of a star player in The Hundred tournament. Today’s mystery cricketer is the fourth player featured in the daily guessing game. More clues will unlock with each incorrect guess until the correct answer is revealed.

stories.title: ‘Jumbo’ Sequel, ‘Queen of Malacca’ Franchise Plans on the Cards as Indonesia’s Visinema Eyes Expansion (EXCLUSIVE)
stories.summary: Indonesian studio Visinema plans to release the fantasy epic Queen of Malacca in early 2027, following post-production progress and rising international interest from distributors in China and Thailand. The sequel to the hit Jumbo underscores Visinema’s push to expand its regional franchise reach.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: Andy Burnham will hold his first meeting at the new No 10 North office in Manchester today, marking a push to decentralise government power from London. Up to 300 civil servants will relocate to the North West by next year as part of the prime minister’s devolution plan. The National Economic Council will meet in Manchester to prioritise regional investment and local economic growth.

stories.title: Faced With Heat and Wildfires, France Turns to Spain to Learn How to Cope
stories.summary: A French government delegation will travel to its southern neighbor to learn how it copes with long spells of hot weather.

stories.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
stories.summary: A rare black-crowned night heron, typically found in Africa, Asia, and the Americas, has been spotted at the National Trust’s Holnicote Estate in Somerset. The sighting follows conservation efforts to restore freshwater habitats, benefiting species like water voles, otters, and white-tailed eagles. Conservationists say the UK’s wetlands have declined by over 97%, making such sightings significant for wildlife recovery.

stories.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
stories.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

stories.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
stories.summary: A Hampshire science centre has opened a new play area to teach children about England’s rare chalk streams, which face pressure from hot and dry weather. The Winchester Science Centre’s exhibit lets kids explore why water flow and temperature matter to these unique habitats. The project aims to inspire future conservation efforts as wildlife groups push for stronger protections.

stories.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
stories.summary: A US study found replacing gas stoves with electric models cut hospital admissions and missed work or school by 70% for people with poorly controlled asthma. The Cleveland-area trial, involving 72 homes, showed benefits comparable to or exceeding common asthma medications. Funding was halted early, leaving over 1,100 households without access to the program.

stories.title: Female veterans support group makes national impact
stories.summary: Chelley Tulloch, a former RAF medic, says her PTSD improved after joining a female veterans support group following multiple war-zone deployments. The group, now expanding nationwide, provides tailored mental health help as more women enter the military. The UK Ministry of Defence confirms increased investment in NHS veteran services.

stories.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
stories.summary: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.

stories.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
stories.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: Google has been fined €890 million by the European Union for abusing its market dominance by favouring its own apps over rivals. The penalty, the first under the EU’s Digital Markets Act, follows two breaches where Google gave its services an unfair advantage. Google warns the ruling could strip key features from its search tools and weaken security on Google Play.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

stories.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
stories.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

The week will conclude with shows from heavy-hitters Dolce & Gabbana, Bottega Veneta, and Ferragamo on September 25.
